text: Why Judy matters

text:
Judy
A self-learning system of judges and juries
textPreview: Judy | A self-learning system of judges and juries

text: A deck on the real gap in LLM-as-a-judge, why that gap matters, and why Judy is a credible way to close it.
textPreview: A deck on the real gap in LLM-as-a-judge, why that gap matters, and why Judy is a credible way to close it.

text: Core claim
textPreview: Core claim

text:
The goal is not to build one more judge.

The goal is to build an evaluator that can learn from failure, test whether that learning is real, and eventually move from a single verdict to structured deliberation.
textPreview: The goal is not to build one more judge. |  | The goal is to build an evaluator that can learn from failure, test whether that learning i...

text: Judy
textPreview: Judy

text: The real gap
textPreview: The real gap

text: Many LLM judges look strong on averages and still fail where evaluation matters most.
textPreview: Many LLM judges look strong on averages and still fail where evaluation matters most.

text: Averages hide weakness
textPreview: Averages hide weakness

text: Easy subsets saturate, so a single benchmark number can make a weak judge look reliable.
textPreview: Easy subsets saturate, so a single benchmark number can make a weak judge look reliable.

text: Fluency masks wrongness
textPreview: Fluency masks wrongness

text: Polished answers often win even when they violate the spec, miss evidence, or make unsafe moves.
textPreview: Polished answers often win even when they violate the spec, miss evidence, or make unsafe moves.

text: Consistency is not learning
textPreview: Consistency is not learning

text: A judge agreeing with itself more does not prove it became more correct.
textPreview: A judge agreeing with itself more does not prove it became more correct.

text: What the current baseline already tells us
textPreview: What the current baseline already tells us

text: On a 35-item RewardBench sample, the vanilla judge reaches 88.6% overall agreement. But on `llmbar-adver-neighbor`, it drops to 50%: chance performance on fluent-but-wrong cases.
textPreview: On a 35-item RewardBench sample, the vanilla judge reaches 88.6% overall agreement. But on `llmbar-adver-neighbor`, it drops to 50%: chan...

text: Judy
textPreview: Judy

text: What Judy does differently
textPreview: What Judy does differently

text: Judy treats evaluation as a learning loop, not a one-shot model call.
textPreview: Judy treats evaluation as a learning loop, not a one-shot model call.

text:
Core ablation:
anchored vs. unanchored
textPreview: Core ablation: | anchored vs. unanchored

text: 1. Evaluate
textPreview: 1. Evaluate

text: Judge two answers against the actual prompt, constraints, and format rules..
textPreview: Judge two answers against the actual prompt, constraints, and format rules..

text: 2. Analyze failures
textPreview: 2. Analyze failures

text: Inspect where the judge was fooled and which task-general lesson would have prevented the error..
textPreview: Inspect where the judge was fooled and which task-general lesson would have prevented the error..

text: 3. Rewrite policy
textPreview: 3. Rewrite policy

text: Update the judging skill, not the answer under review..
textPreview: Update the judging skill, not the answer under review..

text: 4. Re-test on held-out
textPreview: 4. Re-test on held-out

text: Measure whether the new policy improves on unseen task types instead of only sounding smarter..
textPreview: Measure whether the new policy improves on unseen task types instead of only sounding smarter..

text: Judy
textPreview: Judy

text: Why this approach is defensible
textPreview: Why this approach is defensible

text: Judy is useful because it measures real evaluator improvement instead of polishing model theater.
textPreview: Judy is useful because it measures real evaluator improvement instead of polishing model theater.

text: External anchor
textPreview: External anchor

text: Improvement is checked against known ordering or benchmark labels, not just self-consistency.
textPreview: Improvement is checked against known ordering or benchmark labels, not just self-consistency.

text: Unseen task types
textPreview: Unseen task types

text: The held-out split contains task types the judge never learns from during policy updates.
textPreview: The held-out split contains task types the judge never learns from during policy updates.

text: Bias-aware metrics
textPreview: Bias-aware metrics

text: Agreement alone is not enough. Judy also tracks position-consistency and score-spread.
textPreview: Agreement alone is not enough. Judy also tracks position-consistency and score-spread.

text: Method spectrum
textPreview: Method spectrum

text: The system starts with cheap, legible context improvement and creates a clean baseline for later tuning and jury methods.
textPreview: The system starts with cheap, legible context improvement and creates a clean baseline for later tuning and jury methods.

text: Judy
textPreview: Judy

title: Why Judy matters beyond this demo

text: Why Judy matters beyond this demo
textPreview: Why Judy matters beyond this demo

text: A better evaluator is leverage: it improves how future systems are trained, reviewed, and trusted.
textPreview: A better evaluator is leverage: it improves how future systems are trained, reviewed, and trusted.

text: Better model iteration
textPreview: Better model iteration

text: If the evaluator is weak, every downstream improvement loop is noisy or misdirected.
textPreview: If the evaluator is weak, every downstream improvement loop is noisy or misdirected.

text: More trustworthy benchmarking
textPreview: More trustworthy benchmarking

text: Hard-case-aware evaluation prevents teams from overclaiming progress based on easy averages.
textPreview: Hard-case-aware evaluation prevents teams from overclaiming progress based on easy averages.

text: Lower human review cost
textPreview: Lower human review cost

text: A judge that knows when it is weak can route fewer, better cases to human review.
textPreview: A judge that knows when it is weak can route fewer, better cases to human review.

text: Path to richer decision-making
textPreview: Path to richer decision-making

text: The judge-and-jury vision opens the door to multiple perspectives: correctness, helpfulness, preference, and bias.
textPreview: The judge-and-jury vision opens the door to multiple perspectives: correctness, helpfulness, preference, and bias.

text: The roadmap is built to move from legible evaluation improvement to frontier evaluator systems.
textPreview: The roadmap is built to move from legible evaluation improvement to frontier evaluator systems.

textPreview: Now

text: Ship the live API, replace mock UI data, and deepen hard-subset benchmarking.
textPreview: Ship the live API, replace mock UI data, and deepen hard-subset benchmarking.

text: Next
textPreview: Next

text: Run Gemini tuning baselines and test whether modest weight updates outperform prompt-only improvement.
textPreview: Run Gemini tuning baselines and test whether modest weight updates outperform prompt-only improvement.

text: Then
textPreview: Then

text: Add stronger bias probes and selective grounding on uncertain or claim-heavy cases.
textPreview: Add stronger bias probes and selective grounding on uncertain or claim-heavy cases.

text: Later
textPreview: Later

text: Move into judge-and-jury deliberation, preference learning, and active learning for human feedback.

title: Judy matters because better AI systems need better evaluators.

text: Judy matters because better AI systems need better evaluators.

text: If we can make the judge learn, measure that learning honestly, and extend it toward structured deliberation, we improve the whole stack built on top of it.
